headlines.title: High Oil Prices Are Driving an EV Boom in Europe
headlines.summary: High oil and fuel prices led to yet another monthly rise in electric car sales in Europe, at a solid 34% on the year, thanks in no small part to the wider availability of cheaper vehicles from China, Reuters reported today, citing data from E-Mobility and New Automotive. headlines.title: Oil Prices Slide After U.S. and Iran Sign Ceasefire Agreement
headlines.summary: Oil prices were under pressure in early Asian trading on Thursday after the U.S. and Iran formally signed an agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: The Invisible Energy Crisis Threatening to Derail the AI Boom
headlines.summary: Every AI boom forecast being published right now — every bull case, Big Tech earnings call, and valuation model — seems to be making the same assumption. headlines.title: State of Global Oil Inventories Ruins Iran Peace Optimism
headlines.summary: As oil benchmarks crash and Brent slips below $80 per barrel, a growing number of analysts are sounding an alarm: Hormuz may reopen, but oil production in the region would not rebound immediately—and the world’s oil inventories are depleting. headlines.title: The Diesel Demand Shock Nobody Is Pricing In
headlines.summary: The Truck Exception Isn't as Big as You Think When I recently argued that the internal combustion engine may already have passed its peak, many readers responded with a familiar objection. headlines.title: Clean Energy Investment Hits $2.2 Trillion, Nearly Double Fossil Fuels
headlines.summary: Despite historic political headwinds against the global decarbonization effort, clean energy spending continues to see a meteoric rise around the world. headlines.title: How the Oil Sands Became the Lowest-Cost North American Producer
headlines.summary: After the oil price crash of 2014-15, global energy majors like BP (NYSE:BP), Chevron (NYSE:CVX), and TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) sold their interests in the Canadian oil sands, at the time classifying their Canadian operations as among the most expensive and least profitable. headlines.title: IEA Sees Massive Oil Surplus In 2027 As Middle East Supply Returns
headlines.summary: The global oil market could swing from one of the largest supply disruptions in history to a surplus of more than 5 million barrels per day next year if Middle East production and exports recover following the U.S.-Iran peace agreement, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said

headlines.title: Artificial Intelligence Is Driving a Geothermal Energy Boom
headlines.summary: The artificial intelligence boom is fueling an energy revolution.
